citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
A female shih tzu (#078; DOB 8/3/21), randomly pulled for examination, had a loose upper premolar on the right side of its
mouth. In addition, the dog was drooling and there was a significant amount of tartar and gunk on both sides of the mouth.
The dog was difficult to examine and kept pulling away as the inspector was trying to take a closer look at her mouth.
Dental issues can be painful and can lead to infection which can become systemic. Daily observation must be done for all
animals to assess their health and well-being and a mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending
veterinarian. The attending veterinarian must be contacted and the dog must be treated as per his/her instructions.
Correct by 4/30/26.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The licensee stated the outdoor runs haven't been sanitized at all and the indoor kennels get sanitized about once every
four months. The doggy doors had grime built up on them and hair was accumulating on top of the outdoor enclosures.
Not sanitizing the enclosures on a biweekly basis allows for grime to build up which can contribute to the spread of
disease. The licensee must sanitize the enclosures every two weeks. Correct by 5/11/26.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
End of report.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
A female shih tzu (#078; DOB 8/3/21), randomly pulled for examination, had a loose upper premolar on the right side of its 
mouth. In addition, the dog was drooling and there was a significant amount of tartar and gunk on both sides of the mouth. 
The dog was difficult to examine and kept pulling away as the inspector was trying to take a closer look at her mouth. 
Dental issues can be painful and can lead to infection which can become systemic. Daily observation must be done for all 
animals to assess their health and well-being and a mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that 
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending 
veterinarian. The attending veterinarian must be contacted and the dog must be treated as per his/her instructions. 
Correct by 4/30/26. 
3.11(b)(2) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The licensee stated the outdoor runs haven't been sanitized at all and the indoor kennels get sanitized about once every 
four months. The doggy doors had grime built up on them and hair was accumulating on top of the outdoor enclosures. 
Not sanitizing the enclosures on a biweekly basis allows for grime to build up which can contribute to the spread of 
disease. The licensee must sanitize the enclosures every two weeks. Correct by 5/11/26. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee. 
 
End of report. 
 
 n
